attachment.content:
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

15	.. {rbbc_fatal}

19	. *Clean Previous Instance*:
20	.. {rbbc_call}
21	`docker compose ... down --remove-orphans` to remove any existing containers and networks for this nameplate's compose project
22	.. Tolerates missing project (first start)
23	
24	//axhos_step
25	. *Reclaim Nameplate Subnet*:
26	.. Enumerate all Docker networks and inspect each for subnet match against `{rbrn_enclave_base_ip}/{rbrn_enclave_netmask}`; skip this nameplate's own compose network (already removed by the prior step)
27	.. A crucible's subnet derives from the nameplate, not from any project prefix, so at most one network can occupy it; freeing the subnet is the whole invariant and needs no name-lineage check
28	.. {rbbc_warn}
29	on the occupying network, if any: force-remove its attached containers, then remove the network — reclaiming the subnet regardless of the network's prefix or how it was created (compose or manual `docker network create`)
30	.. {rbbc_fatal}
31	if the occupying network cannot be removed

37	.. Compose creates networks, starts sentry, waits for health, starts pentacle, waits for health, starts bottle

1	//axhob_operation
2	{opss_sentry_start}
3	
4	NOTE: Docker Compose orchestrates the full container lifecycle declaratively.
5	The sentry starts first; its health check (`/tmp/rbjh_healthy`) gates the pentacle; the pentacle's health check (`/tmp/rbjph_healthy`) gates the bottle.
6	Network creation, container instantiation, and startup ordering are all managed by compose — the sub-operations below describe what happens inside each container at startup, not imperative orchestration steps.
7	
8	//axhos_step
9	. *Compose creates networks and starts sentry*:
10	.. Compose creates
11	{at_transit_network}
12	(`transit`) and
13	{at_enclave_network}
14	(`enclave` with static subnet from nameplate)
15	.. Sentry container starts on both networks with privileged mode
16	.. Baked-in entrypoint (`rbjs_sentry.sh`) executes
17	
18	//axhos_step
19	. *Sentry discovers interfaces and takes ownership of routing*:
20	.. Sentry uses `ip -o addr show to «enclave_ip»` to identify which interface carries the enclave IP
21	.. The other global-scope interface is identified as the uplink
22	.. All iptables rules use discovered interface names, not hardcoded eth0/eth1
23	.. NOTE: Docker Compose does not guarantee network-to-interface ordering; IP-based discovery is required
24	.. Sentry replaces the default route with one via the uplink gateway, computed as the first usable IP of the uplink subnet (Docker bridge convention)
25	.. NOTE: Docker assigns the default route to the alphabetically-first attached network's gateway (empirically, on both linux engine 28.x and mac Docker Desktop 28.x); compose `services.<svc>.networks.<net>.priority` does NOT influence this. Sentry-side ownership is the load-bearing fix — same don't-trust-Docker discipline that motivates IP-based interface discovery.
26	.. NOTE: Sentry takes ownership of any runtime-implicit behavior its security envelope depends on.
27	   This applies to interface naming (IP-based discovery, above), default-route selection (explicit override post-discovery, above), published-port target selection (iptables rules that do not depend on which interface the framework chose for delivery), and any future runtime-implicit ordering that emerges.
28	   Kernel state and sentry's own iptables are the source of truth; framework-implicit ordering is not load-bearing for the security envelope.
29	
30	//axhos_step
31	. *Sentry configures iptables*:
32	.. Default policies: DROP on INPUT, FORWARD, OUTPUT
33	.. Custom chains: RBM-INGRESS, RBM-EGRESS, RBM-FORWARD
34	.. ICMP allowed within enclave only; ICMP cross-boundary blocked
35	.. Entry-port DNAT plus POSTROUTING MASQUERADE and conntrack-DNAT-state FORWARD authorization if {rbrn_entry_mode} is enabled — see {scr_port_setup} for the iptables rules and kernel sysctls (rp_filter loose, ip_local_port_range) the entry-port path requires
36	.. Access and DNS rules per nameplate configuration (allowlist/global/disabled modes)
37	
38	//axhos_step
39	. *Sentry starts dnsmasq*:
40	.. Listens on enclave interface at
41	{rbrn_enclave_sentry_ip}
42	.. Domain filtering per
43	{rbrn_uplink_dns_mode}
44	(allowlist or global)
45	.. DNS firewall rules added to RBM-INGRESS and RBM-EGRESS
46	
47	//axhos_step
48	. *Sentry signals health*:
49	.. Creates `/tmp/rbjh_healthy`
50	.. Compose health check detects this and allows pentacle to start
51	
52	//axhoc_completion
53	After sentry is healthy, the
54	{at_sentry_container}
55	has dual network connectivity with iptables enforcement and DNS services active, ready to serve the
56	{rbsc_crucible}.

1	==== Multi-Architecture Strategy Caveat
2	
3	All sections below assume Google Cloud Build (GCB) remains the sole builder.
4	GCB provides only x86 workers — default and private pools alike.
5	Multi-platform builds use QEMU emulation via the binfmt setup step (rbgjb02).
6	The binfmt step is conditionally included: vessels whose `RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS` matches the build runner platform (`RBGC_BUILD_RUNNER_PLATFORM`) skip it, reducing attack surface for native-only builds.
7	
8	If a vessel requires native ARM builds, the builder strategy must expand beyond GCB, and further GCB-specific hardening should be re-evaluated.
9	
10	If native ARM builds become necessary, builder-agnostic controls (cosign signing, portable SLSA provenance) become more valuable than GCB-specific infrastructure (private pools, VPC Service Controls).
11	See cosign deferral rationale in Deferred Items.
12	
13	==== Current Posture
14	
15	Properties of the build pipeline as of 2026-03.
16	Authoritative procedures live in the operational specs (RBSAC, RBSAV, RBSDC, RBSDN);
17	this section states what holds and why it matters.
18	
19	**Build definitions are fully static.**
20	Each vessel's `cloudbuild.json` is stitched from step scripts (`rbgjb*.sh`) and current regime pins -- zero custom substitution overrides at submission time.
21	The build is fully determined by the stitched JSON and the pouch (build context as OCI image in GAR).
22	See RBSAC.
23	
24	**SLSA v1.0 Build Level 3 provenance — verified.**
25	All `builds.create` submissions produce SLSA v1.0 Build Level 3 provenance via `requestedVerifyOption: VERIFIED` and the `images:` field declaring per-platform image URIs.
26	Google Container Analysis generates both v0.1 and v1 predicate attestations per image.
27	Provenance chain: stitched JSON + pouch -> per-platform image digest.
28	Verification via DSSE envelope signature verification (jq + openssl against embedded GCB attestor keys).
29	
30	Production-verified build IDs:
31	
32	* Single-arch: `16d3b60f` (busybox-amd64), `fc36b970` (busybox-arm64), `9180c42a` (trbim-macos)
33	* Multi-platform: `2e172ce0` (rbev-busybox, 3 platforms — amd64, arm64, armv7)
34	
35	**Security boundary: pouch and stitched JSON.**
36	Cloud Build receives build context via the pouch (FROM SCRATCH OCI image in GAR) -- per-vessel Dockerfile and context files.
37	The main repo (tooling, specs, regime files) is never exposed to Google.
38	The Director controls what enters the pouch; the cloud is just labor.
39	See RBSAC.
40	
41	**Builder step images are reliquary-sourced.**
42	All GCB step images are pulled from a datestamped reliquary in GAR — co-versioned tool images frozen by the inscribe ceremony.
43	`RBRV_RELIQUARY` in each vessel regime identifies the active reliquary.
44	Base images are separately enshrined via `RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR`.
45	
46	**Dual private pool workers (tether + airgap).**
47	Every depot gets two private pools: a tether pool (public egress) and an airgap pool (`NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS`).
48	Pool routing is operation-level: inscribe and enshrine use tether (upstream access required), about and vouch always use airgap (verification isolated from public registries), conjure and bind use the vessel's `RBRV_EGRESS_MODE`.
49	Pools are fully managed by Google (scale to zero, no VMs to maintain).
50	
51	**Artifact Registry storage.**
52	Built images are stored in Google Artifact Registry (GAR), not Docker Hub or other external registries.
53	Regime variables configure project, region, and repository.
54	
55	**Dedicated Mason service account.**
56	Scoped to depot-project permissions, specified in the `serviceAccount` field of each build JSON.
57	
58	**SBOM generation.**
59	Per-platform Syft scans via `registry:` transport (reads directly from GAR using an OAuth2 token obtained from the GCB metadata server — no Docker daemon coupling).
60	Each platform image gets its own JSON and table-format SBOM, packaged into the per-platform metadata container.
61	Aligned with CISA 2025 minimum elements and EU CRA requirements.
62	The upgrade path is runtime-aware analysis, not removal.
63	
64	**Cloud Build logging.**
65	Build requests set `options.logging: CLOUD_LOGGING_ONLY`.
66	Log hygiene (no credential echo) was audited during trigger migration script inlining.
67	
68	**Provenance on demand.**
69	`rbfl_tally()` lists all hallmarks and queries Container Analysis inline for each per-platform image, displaying SLSA build level, build invocation ID, and single-origin verification per hallmark.
70	Tabtarget:
71	{rbtgo_hallmark_tally}.
72	
73	===== Pipeline Architecture: Single-Platform Vessels
74	
75	Five build steps plus CB-native `images:` push (binfmt step excluded at stitch time).
76	Applies to vessels whose `RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS` matches `RBGC_BUILD_RUNNER_PLATFORM`.
77	
78	1. **derive-tag-base** — compute timestamps and tag components from CB substitutions; log build strategy
79	2. **build-and-load** — `docker buildx build --load` (single platform into local daemon)
80	3. **sbom** — Syft scan via `registry:` transport
81	4. **assemble-metadata** — per-platform `build_info.json` with SLSA summary fields
82	5. **build-and-push-metadata** — multi-platform `about` container pushed to `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/<hallmark>/about:<hallmark>` via `buildx --push`
83	
84	`images:` field declares the single per-platform URI → CB pushes and generates SLSA Level 3.
85	
86	===== Pipeline Architecture: Multi-Platform Vessels
87	
88	Seven build steps plus CB-native `images:` push (binfmt step included at stitch time).
89	Applies to vessels whose `RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS` differs from `RBGC_BUILD_RUNNER_PLATFORM`.
90	About steps (`rbgja/`) are embedded in the build job via stitch (combined image+about).
91	Vouch (`rbgjv/`) runs as a separate Cloud Build job.
92	
93	1. **derive-tag-base** — compute timestamps and tag components; log build strategy
94	2. **qemu-binfmt** — register QEMU user-static for cross-arch emulation
95	3. **buildx-push-multi** — `docker buildx build --push` all platforms to intermediate `-multi` tag; capture `cache_before.json` (Docker daemon state pre-build) and `buildkit_metadata.json` (via `--metadata-file`)
96	4. **per-platform-pullback** — `docker pull --platform` each arch, tag individually
97	5. **push-per-platform** — `docker push` each per-platform tag (pre-push for imagetools); capture `cache_after.json` (Docker daemon state post-push)
98	6. **imagetools-create** — reassemble consumer-facing manifest list from per-platform tags
99	7. **push-diags** — build `FROM scratch` container with build-time diagnostic files (`buildkit_metadata.json`, `cache_before.json`, `cache_after.json`, `recipe.txt`), push as `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/diags:«HALLMARK»`
100	
101	`images:` field declares all per-platform URIs → CB re-pushes (idempotent) and generates SLSA Level 3.
102	The reassembled manifest list provides transparent platform resolution to attested per-platform images.
103	
104	===== Tag Scheme
105	
106	[cols="1,2",options="header"]
107	|===
108	|Tag Pattern
109	|Purpose
110	
111	|`{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/{HALLMARK}/image:{INSCRIBE_TS}-multi`
112	|Intermediate `buildx --push` target (multi-platform only)
113	
114	|`{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/{HALLMARK}/image:{HALLMARK}-{SUFFIX}`
115	|Per-platform image tag on the image package (persistent, hallmark-tagged)
116	
117	|`{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/{HALLMARK}/image:{INSCRIBE_TS}-{SUFFIX}`
118	|SLSA alias tag (`images:` field, provenance generation trigger — ephemeral, overwritten on re-conjure)
119	
120	|`{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/{HALLMARK}/image:{HALLMARK}`
121	|Consumer-facing manifest list on the image package (reassembled by `imagetools create`, multi-platform only)
122	
123	|`{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/{HALLMARK}/about:{HALLMARK}`
124	|Metadata container on the about package (build provenance — multi-platform, see About Artifact Contents below)
125	
126	|`{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/{HALLMARK}/diags:{HALLMARK}`
127	|Build-time diagnostics container on the diags package (conjure only — `buildkit_metadata.json`, `cache_before.json`, `cache_after.json`, `recipe.txt`). Pushed by conjure step 07, pulled by about step 01.
128	
129	|`{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/{HALLMARK}/attest:{HALLMARK}-{SUFFIX}`
130	|Per-platform attestation tags on the single attest package (one tag per architecture).
131	|===
132	
133	Platform suffix derivation: `linux/amd64` → `amd64`, `linux/arm64` → `arm64`, `linux/arm/v7` → `armv7`.
134	Computed at inscribe time from `RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS`.
135	
136	===== About Artifact Contents
137	
138	The `about` artifact (at `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/<hallmark>/about:<hallmark>`) is a `FROM scratch` multi-platform container carrying build provenance files at the root filesystem.
139	Contents are extracted by
140	{rbtgo_ark_inspect}
141	for trust posture display.
142	
143	[cols="1,2",options="header"]
144	|===
145	|File
146	|Contents
147	
148	|`build_info.json`
149	|Per-platform build metadata: hallmark, image URI, moniker, platform, QEMU usage, git coordinates (repo/branch/commit), build timestamps, and SLSA summary (build level, invocation ID, builder ID, predicate types).
150	
151	|`sbom.json`
152	|Syft SPDX JSON output: package inventory with type, name, version, license, and Package URL for every detected package.
153	
154	|`recipe.txt`
155	|The exact Dockerfile used to build the image.
156	
157	|`buildkit_metadata.json`
158	|BuildKit resolved base image references and build parameters (conjure only, forwarded from the `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/<hallmark>/diags` package).
159	
160	|`cache_before.json`
161	|Docker daemon image state before the build (conjure only, forwarded from the `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/<hallmark>/diags` package).
162	
163	|`cache_after.json`
164	|Docker daemon image state after all pushes (conjure only, forwarded from the `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/<hallmark>/diags` package).
165	
166	|===
167	
168	NOTE: The three enrichment files (`buildkit_metadata.json`, `cache_before.json`, `cache_after.json`) are present only for conjure hallmarks.
169	They are forwarded from the `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/<hallmark>/diags` registry package by the about pipeline's step 1.
170	For bind and graft, these files are absent and the about artifact contains only `build_info.json`, `sbom.json`, and optionally `recipe.txt`.
171	
172	===== OCI Layout Bridge — Superseded
173	
174	The original OCI Layout Bridge (RBSOB) — export to OCI tarball, push via crane, split with skopeo — is fully superseded for all vessel types.
175	Single-platform vessels use `buildx --load` into the local daemon.
176	Multi-platform vessels use `buildx --push` with per-platform pullback.
177	CB pre-populates GAR credentials; no explicit docker login step is needed.
178	See RBSOB superseded notice.
179	
180	==== Implemented: Egress Lockdown via Dual Pools
181	
182	Egress lockdown is implemented through the dual-pool architecture.
183	The airgap pool (`NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS`) blocks all public internet access.
184	Operations that require upstream access (inscribe, enshrine) run on the tether pool.
185	Operations that must be isolated (about, vouch) always run on the airgap pool.
186	Conjure and bind are routed per vessel's `RBRV_EGRESS_MODE`.
187	
188	**Dependency mirroring is universal.**
189	All GCB step images come from reliquaries (inscribed tool images in GAR).
190	All base images come from anchors (enshrined base images in GAR).
191	This is not just an airgap feature — it is a universal robustness measure against upstream registry churn.
192	
193	**Operation pool routing:**
194	
195	[cols="1,1,2",options="header"]
196	|===
197	|Operation
198	|Pool
199	|Rationale
200	
201	|Inscribe
202	|tether
203	|Pulls tool images from upstream registries
204	
205	|Enshrine
206	|tether
207	|Pulls base images from upstream via skopeo
208	
209	|Conjure/Bind
210	|vessel's RBRV_EGRESS_MODE
211	|Build-time deps vary per vessel
212	
213	|About
214	|airgap (always)
215	|Only needs GAR + Google APIs (Private Google Access)
216	
217	|Vouch
218	|airgap (always)
219	|Verification isolated from public registries
220	|===
221	
222	**About/vouch always-airgap is a security property**: verification can never be influenced by a compromised public registry, even for tether vessels.
223	
224	==== Potential Hardening: VPC Service Controls Perimeter
225	
226	Wrap the depot project in a VPC Service Controls (VPC-SC) perimeter.
227	This restricts which GCP APIs can be called and from where, reducing data exfiltration risk.
228	
229	**Required infrastructure:**
230	
231	* VPC-SC perimeter around the depot project (organizational policy — may require org-level admin)
232	* Access levels for operator workstations (so builds can still be submitted from outside the perimeter)
233	* Ingress/egress rules for the perimeter (which services may cross the boundary)
234	* Testing: every GCP API call in the pipeline must be verified inside the perimeter — Cloud Logging writes, GCS uploads, Artifact Registry pushes, Cloud Build API calls can break subtly
235	
236	**Prerequisite:** Egress Lockdown is in place (dual-pool architecture).
237	
238	==== Deferred Items
239	
240	Evaluated and consciously deferred; each has a specific revisit trigger.
241	
242	**Dockerfile base image digest validation.**
243	Builder step images are digest-pinned (Current Posture), but user-authored Dockerfiles in vessel build contexts are not validated.
244	A vessel with `FROM ubuntu:22.04` passes today — mutable tag, supply-chain risk.
245	This constrains vessel authors (policy decision with RBSRV implications), orthogonal to pipeline infrastructure.
246	Separate future heat.
247	
248	**BuildKit provenance flag.**
249	`--provenance=true` on `docker buildx build` records builder internals — useful for deeper forensics.
250	GCB-native SLSA v1.0 provenance covers the primary attestation use case.
251	Redundant for the current threat model.
252	Revisit if build reproducibility verification becomes a priority.
253	
254	**Cosign image signing.**
255	Cryptographic signatures attached to images, verifiable by downstream consumers.
256	Two modes: keyless (Sigstore/Fulcio — requires public internet, conflicts with Egress Lockdown) and keyed (Cloud KMS — no external dependency).
257	With CB-native SLSA v1.0 Build Level 3 verified on all vessel types, cosign is redundant for the current threat model (building and consuming within own GCP project).
258	Becomes valuable if: images are distributed externally, trust verification needed outside GCP, or builder strategy expands beyond GCB (losing Google's provenance).
259	If adopted, prefer KMS-backed keyed signing (`cosign sign --key gcpkms://...`) for Egress Lockdown compatibility.
260	
261	**Binary Authorization.**
262	Deploy-time policy enforcement: only attested images can be deployed.
263	Relevant for GKE and Cloud Run.
264	Now technically achievable — CB-native SLSA Level 3 satisfies BinAuth attestation requirements.
265	Recipe Bottle deploys to Podman on workstations — no benefit until deployment targets change.
266	
267	**Reproducibility verification.**
268	Periodic independent rebuilds compared by digest.
269	Requires reproducible toolchains — a vessel-author discipline, not a pipeline control.
270	Not actionable until vessel ecosystem matures.
271	
272	==== References
273	
274	**Google Cloud documentation:**
275	
276	* https://docs.google.com/build/docs/securing-builds/generate-validate-build-provenance[Generate and validate build provenance (SLSA)]
277	* https://docs.google.com/build/docs/private-pools/private-pools-overview[Private Pools overview]
278	* https://docs.google.com/build/docs/private-pools/private-pool-config-file-schema[Private Pool configuration schema]
279	* https://docs.google.com/build/docs/private-pools/use-in-private-network[Cloud Build in a private network (NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS)]
280	* https://docs.google.com/build/docs/private-pools/using-vpc-service-controls[VPC Service Controls with Cloud Build]
281	* https://cloud.google.com/binary-authorization/docs/deploy-cloud-build[Deploy only images built by Cloud Build (Binary Authorization)]
282	* https://cloud.google.com/build/pricing-update[Cloud Build pricing update (2025)]
283	
284	**SBOM and supply chain:**
285	
286	* https://www.cisa.gov/resources-tools/resources/2025-minimum-elements-software-bill-materials-sbom[CISA 2025 Minimum Elements for SBOM]
287	* https://www.cisa.gov/sbom[CISA SBOM overview]
288	* https://openssf.org/blog/2025/06/05/choosing-an-sbom-generation-tool/[OpenSSF: Choosing an SBOM Generation Tool]
289	
290	**Signing and attestation:**
291	
292	* https://docs.sigstore.dev/cosign/signing/signing_with_containers/[Cosign container signing]
293	* https://slsa.dev/provenance/v1[SLSA Provenance v1.0 specification]
294	
295	**Internal references:**
296	
297	* RBSOB: OCI Layout Bridge trade study (superseded)
298	* RBSCJ: Cloud Build JSON trade study
299	* RBSTB: Trigger Build specification (inactive -- triggers eliminated, builds.create replaces)
300	* rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh: `zrbfd_stitch_build_json()` — build stitching; `rbfl_tally()` — provenance on demand; `rbfc_plumb_full()`/`rbfc_plumb_compact()` — local trust posture display
301	* RBSAI: Ark Inspect specification
302	* buv_validation.sh: `buv_val_odref()` — digest-pinned image reference validator
303	* RBRV vessel regime: `RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS` vs `RBGC_BUILD_RUNNER_PLATFORM` — binfmt inclusion predicate
304	* Memos/memo-20260305-provenance-architecture-gap.md — provenance experiment evidence

4	This regime specifies variables that define crucible deployment configurations within the Recipe Bottle system.
5	Each
6	{rbrn_regime}
7	instance represents a distinct service deployment with its own network, entry, and uplink settings.

1	//axhrb_regime
2	{rbrv_regime}
3	
4	This regime specifies variables that define container vessel configurations within the Recipe Bottle system.
5	Each
6	{rbrv_regime}
7	instance represents a vessel image source: bound from an external registry, conjured from a Dockerfile, or grafted from a local build.
8	RBRV assignment files are found one per vessel subdirectory under {rbrr_vessel_dir} and configure image sourcing for bottle deployments.
9	
10	//axhrv_variable
11	{rbrv_sigil}
12	
13	Unique identifier for the vessel.
14	Must be 1-64 characters forming a valid cross-context identifier.
15	Must match the vessel directory name.
16	
17	//axhrv_variable
18	{rbrv_description}
19	
20	Human-readable description of the vessel purpose.
21	Optional; 0-512 characters.
22	
23	//axhrv_variable
24	{rbrv_user}
25	
26	Runtime user for the vessel container.
27	Optional; 0-64 characters.
28	When set, declares the non-root user that downstream orchestration (compose user directive, SSH authentication, exec sessions) should target.
29	When unset, the container runs as its image default.
30	Applies across all vessel modes: conjure vessels create the user in their Dockerfile; bind and graft vessels declare a user that already exists in the image.
31	
32	//axhrv_variable
33	{rbrv_vessel_mode}
34	
35	Selects vessel provisioning strategy.
36	Must be
37	{rbrv_vessel_mode_bind},
38	{rbrv_vessel_mode_conjure},
39	or
40	{rbrv_vessel_mode_graft}.
41

43	{rbrv_egress_mode}
44	
45	Selects which worker pool the primary build operation uses.
46	Must be `tether` (public egress) or `airgap` (`NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS`).
47	Inscribe and enshrine always use tether regardless of this setting.
48	About and vouch always use airgap regardless of this setting.
49	Conjure and bind use the pool selected by this variable.
50	
51	//axhrgb_group
52	{rbrv_group_binding}
53	
54	//axhrgc_gate
55	{rbrv_vessel_mode}
56	{rbrv_vessel_mode_bind}
57	
58	Variables in this group apply only when
59	{rbrv_vessel_mode}
60	is
61	{rbrv_vessel_mode_bind}.
62	Binding retrieves a pre-built image from an external registry without local build steps.
63	
64	//axhrgv_variable
65	{rbrv_bind_image}

75	{rbtgo_ark_about}
76	and embedded in the about artifact as `recipe.txt`.
77	Optional; 0-512 characters.

79	//axhrgb_group
80	{rbrv_group_conjuring}
81	
82	//axhrgc_gate
83	{rbrv_vessel_mode}
84	{rbrv_vessel_mode_conjure}
85	
86	Variables in this group apply only when
87	{rbrv_vessel_mode}
88	is
89	{rbrv_vessel_mode_conjure}.
90	Conjuring builds a vessel image locally from a Dockerfile and build context in the repository.
91	
92	//axhrgv_variable
93	{rbrv_conjure_dockerfile}
94	
95	Path to Dockerfile relative to repository root.
96	Must be 1-512 characters.
97	
98	//axhrgv_variable
99	{rbrv_conjure_bldcontext}
100	
101	Build context path relative to repository root.
102	Must be 1-512 characters.
103	
104	//axhrgv_variable
105	{rbrv_conjure_platforms}
106	
107	Space-separated list of target platforms for multi-platform builds.
108	Must be 1-512 characters.
109	Examples: `linux/amd64`, `linux/amd64 linux/arm64`.
110	
111	//axhrgb_group
112	{rbrv_group_image}
113	
114	//axhrgc_gate
115	{rbrv_vessel_mode}
116	{rbrv_vessel_mode_conjure}
117	
118	Variables in this group declare base image dependencies for multi-stage Dockerfiles.
119	Up to 3 indexed slots (n = 1, 2, 3).
120	{rbtgo_ark_enshrine}
121	resolves each
122	{rbrv_image_origin}
123	to a GAR-mirrored
124	{rbrv_image_anchor}.
125	
126	//axhrgv_variable
127	{rbrv_image_origin}
128	
129	Upstream base image tag declaration (e.g., `python:3.11-slim`).
130	Vessel author declares intent; the {at_foundry} resolves to a concrete reference.
131	Optional; 0-512 characters per slot.

133	//axhrgv_variable
134	{rbrv_image_anchor}
135	
136	GAR-mirrored, content-addressed anchor for the base image.
137	Written by
138	{rbtgo_ark_enshrine}.
139	Format: `{sanitized-origin}-{10-char-sha256}`.
140	When present, both {rbtgo_ark_conjure} and {rbtgo_ark_kludge} substitute the full GAR reference; kludge additionally requires the referenced image to be present in the local container runtime cache (see {rbtgo_image_retrieve} for the credentialed wrest path).
141	When absent, both operations pass
142	{rbrv_image_origin}
143	through as the upstream tag; kludge auto-pulls the origin inline if it's not already cached locally.
144	Optional; 0-512 characters per slot.
145	
146	//axhrgb_group
147	{rbrv_group_grafting}
148	
149	//axhrgc_gate
150	{rbrv_vessel_mode}
151	{rbrv_vessel_mode_graft}
152	
153	Variables in this group apply only when
154	{rbrv_vessel_mode}
155	is
156	{rbrv_vessel_mode_graft}.
157	Grafting accepts a locally-built image pushed to GAR via native docker/podman push.
158	No Cloud Build involvement for the image itself — about and vouch run in Cloud Build after push.
159	
160	//axhrgv_variable
161	{rbrv_graft_image}
162	
163	Local image reference for graft operations.
164	Must be 1-512 characters.
165	Provides the image argument to
166	{rbtgo_ark_graft}.
167	
168	//axhrgv_variable
169	{rbrv_graft_optional_dockerfile}
170	
171	Optional path to Dockerfile used for the local build.
172	When configured, the Dockerfile content is passed to
173	{rbtgo_ark_about}
174	and embedded in the about artifact as `recipe.txt`.
175	Optional; 0-512 characters.
176	
177	//axhro_kindle
178	{rbkro_kindle}
179	
180	Sets defaults for all RBRV_ variables via `VAR="${VAR:-}"` pattern.
181	Detects unexpected RBRV_ variables via compgen prefix scan.
182	Builds ZRBRV_ROLLUP serialization string.
183	
184	//axhro_validate
185	{rbkro_validate}
186	
187	Validates fields via buv_env_* type checkers.
188	Checks mode-appropriate presence invariant: RBRV_BIND_IMAGE for bind, RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE for conjure, optional RBRV_GRAFT_IMAGE for graft.
189	Validates optional Dockerfile paths when configured: RBRV_BIND_OPTIONAL_DOCKERFILE (bind), RBRV_GRAFT_OPTIONAL_DOCKERFILE (graft).
190	
191	//axhro_render
192	{rbkro_render}
193	
194	Renders via rbcr_* shared module.
195	Sections: Core Vessel Identity, Egress Mode, Binding Configuration, Conjuring Configuration, Grafting Configuration.
196	Displays unexpected variables in red.
197	Validates after full render completes.
198	
199	//axhro_list
200	{rbkro_list}
201	
202	Enumerates available vessel instances by scanning subdirectories of RBRR_VESSEL_DIR for {rbrv_regime} files.
203	Returns sigil identifiers suitable for instance selection.

16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# RBOB - Recipe Bottle Orchestration Bottle
20	# Container lifecycle management via Docker Compose
21	#
22	# Requires: buc_command.sh sourced
23	# Requires: rbrn_regime.sh sourced
24	# Requires: rbrr_regime.sh sourced
25	# Requires: rbrd_regime.sh sourced
26	
27	set -euo pipefail
28	
29	# Multiple inclusion detection
30	test -z "${ZRBOB_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbob multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
31	ZRBOB_SOURCED=1
32	
33	
34	######################################################################
35	# Compose-env Validation
36	
37	# Validate that compose-consumed RBRN fields contain no quotes or ${VAR} references.
38	# Compose --env-file does not strip quotes (they become part of the value) and
39	# interpolates ${VAR} references (causing unintended expansion). Fields not consumed
40	# by compose (e.g., RBRN_DESCRIPTION) are exempt.
41	zrbob_validate_compose_env() {
42	  # Explicit list of compose-consumed field names (the nameplate↔compose contract)
43	  local z_compose_fields="
44	    RBRN_MONIKER
45	    RBRN_RUNTIME
46	    RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL
47	    RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL
48	    RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK
49	    RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK
50	    RBRN_ENTRY_MODE
51	    RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION
52	    RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE
53	    RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP
54	    RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK
55	    RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP
56	    RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP
57	    RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN
58	    RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE
59	    RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE
60	    RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS
61	    RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS
62	  "
63	
64	  local z_field=""
65	  for z_field in ${z_compose_fields}; do
66	    local z_value="${!z_field:-}"
67	    # Check for quotes (compose does not strip them)
68	    case "${z_value}" in
69	      *\"*|*\'*)
70	        buc_die "Compose compatibility: ${z_field} contains quotes — compose --env-file does not strip quotes, they become part of the value"
71	        ;;
72	    esac
73	    # Check for ${VAR} references (compose interpolates them)
74	    case "${z_value}" in
75	      *'${'*)
76	        buc_die "Compose compatibility: ${z_field} contains \${VAR} reference — compose --env-file interpolates these, causing unintended expansion"
77	        ;;
78	    esac
79	  done
80	}
81	
82	######################################################################
83	# Kindle and Sentinel
84	
85	zrbob_kindle() {
86	  test -z "${ZRBOB_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbob already kindled"
87	
88	  # Verify RBRN regime is kindled (provides nameplate config)
89	  zrbrn_sentinel
90	
91	  # Validate compose-consumed fields before proceeding
92	  zrbob_validate_compose_env
93	
94	  # Verify RBRR regime is kindled (provides repo config like DNS_SERVER)
95	  zrbrr_sentinel
96	
97	  # Runtime command (docker or podman)
98	  case "${RBRN_RUNTIME}" in
99	    docker) readonly ZRBOB_RUNTIME="docker" ;;
100	    podman) readonly ZRBOB_RUNTIME="podman" ;;
101	    *) buc_die "Unknown RBRN_RUNTIME: ${RBRN_RUNTIME}" ;;
102	  esac
103	
104	  # Compose project identity for this nameplate. The runtime-prefixed moniker
105	  # is the authoritative project name compose labels containers and networks
106	  # under; every -p invocation and every container/network name derives from it.
107	  readonly ZRBOB_PROJECT="${RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX}${RBRN_MONIKER}"
108	
109	  # Container names (for connect and info commands)
110	  readonly ZRBOB_SENTRY="${ZRBOB_PROJECT}-${RBCC_container_sentry}"
111	  readonly ZRBOB_PENTACLE="${ZRBOB_PROJECT}-${RBCC_container_pentacle}"
112	  readonly ZRBOB_BOTTLE="${ZRBOB_PROJECT}-${RBCC_container_bottle}"
113	
114	  # Network name (compose names as {project}_{network}; used by observe and info)
115	  readonly ZRBOB_NETWORK="${ZRBOB_PROJECT}_enclave"
116	
117	  # Base compose file and the compose-quoting probe are kit machinery, not
118	  # consumer config — they live with the kit (RBCC_KIT_DIR), not in moorings.
119	  readonly ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE="${RBCC_KIT_DIR}/rbob_compose.yml"
120	  test -f "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE}" || buc_die "Base compose file not found: ${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE}"
121	
122	  readonly ZRBOB_COMPOSE_FRAGMENT="${RBCC_moorings_dir}/${RBRN_MONIKER}/rbnnh_compose.yml"
123	  # Fragment is optional — existence checked at compose invocation time
124	
125	  readonly ZRBOB_POST_CHARGE_HOOK="${RBCC_moorings_dir}/${RBRN_MONIKER}/rbnnh_post_charge.sh"
126	  # Hook is optional — existence + executable bit checked at charge tail
127	
128	  # Env file paths (for compose --env-file: YAML interpolation + container env forwarding)
129	  readonly ZRBOB_ENV_RBRR="${RBCC_rbrr_file}"
130	  readonly ZRBOB_ENV_RBRD="${RBCC_rbrd_file}"
131	  readonly ZRBOB_ENV_RBJE="${RBCC_KIT_DIR}/rbje_compose_probe.env"
132	  readonly ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN="${RBCC_moorings_dir}/${RBRN_MONIKER}/${RBCC_rbrn_file}"
133	
134	  # RBDC env file — RBDC_* are bash-kindle constants invisible to compose
135	  # without an env-file bridge. Write the subset compose interpolates into
136	  # a temp file and feed it via --env-file.
137	  local z_rbdc_env="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbob_rbdc_compose.env"
138	  printf 'RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID=%s\nRBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY=%s\n' \
139	    "${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}" "${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}" > "${z_rbdc_env}" \
140	    || buc_die "Failed to write RBDC compose env file: ${z_rbdc_env}"
141	  readonly ZRBOB_ENV_RBDC="${z_rbdc_env}"
142	
143	
144	  # GAR image references (computed once, used by preflight and auto-summon)
145	  local z_gar_base="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
146	  readonly ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE="${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE}:${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"
147	  readonly ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE="${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE}:${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"
148	
149	  # GAR vouch references (local presence verified on every start)
150	  readonly ZRBOB_SENTRY_VOUCH="${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH}:${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"
151	  readonly ZRBOB_BOTTLE_VOUCH="${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH}:${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"
152	
153	  # Export RBRN/RBRR vars for compose environment: bare name forwarding.
154	  # Compose --env-file populates the compose environment for both YAML interpolation
155	  # and bare name forwarding; explicit exports provide defense-in-depth.
156	  export RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP
157	  export RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK
158	  export RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP
159	  export RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP
160	  export RBRN_ENTRY_MODE
161	  export RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION
162	  export RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE
163	  export RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE
164	  export RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN
165	  export RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE
166	  export RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS
167	  export RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS
168	  export RBRR_DNS_SERVER
169	  export RBRR_BOTTLE_WORKSPACE
170	
171	  # Host UID/GID for bind-mount ownership alignment.
172	  # The person running charge IS the host identity — no configuration needed.
173	  local z_uid_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbob_host_uid"
174	  local z_gid_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbob_host_gid"
175	  id -u > "${z_uid_file}" || buc_die "Failed to determine host UID"
176	  id -g > "${z_gid_file}" || buc_die "Failed to determine host GID"
177	  local z_host_uid=""
178	  z_host_uid=$(<"${z_uid_file}")
179	  local z_host_gid=""
180	  z_host_gid=$(<"${z_gid_file}")
181	  export RBOB_HOST_UID="${z_host_uid}"
182	  export RBOB_HOST_GID="${z_host_gid}"
183	
184	  # Load bottle vessel user for compose and SSH (optional — empty means image default)
185	  local z_bottle_rbrv="${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}/rbrv.env"
186	  local z_bottle_user=""
187	  if test -f "${z_bottle_rbrv}"; then
188	    z_bottle_user=$(grep '^RBRV_USER=' "${z_bottle_rbrv}" | head -1 | cut -d= -f2) || true
189	  fi
190	  readonly ZRBOB_BOTTLE_USER="${z_bottle_user}"
191	  export RBRV_USER="${z_bottle_user}"
192	
193	  readonly ZRBOB_DRIVE_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbob_drive_"
194	
195	  readonly ZRBOB_KINDLED=1
196	}

198	zrbob_sentinel() {
199	  test "${ZRBOB_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbob not kindled - call zrbob_kindle first"
200	}

202	######################################################################
203	# Compose Command Helper
204	
205	# Build and execute a compose command with standard args
206	# Usage: zrbob_compose <compose_subcommand> [args...]
207	zrbob_compose() {
208	  zrbob_sentinel
209	
210	  local z_args=()
211	  z_args+=("compose")
212	  # Pin the project directory to the moorings (consumer-config) root. Compose
213	  # otherwise derives it from the first -f file's location — and the base
214	  # compose now lives with the kit (Tools/rbk), not in moorings. Nameplate
215	  # fragment (rbnnh_compose.yml) relative paths — env_file, volume mounts —
216	  # are authored relative to this root, so it must be set explicitly. CLI
217	  # --env-file paths resolve against CWD (repo root) and are unaffected.
218	  z_args+=("--project-directory" "${RBCC_moorings_dir}")
219	  z_args+=("--env-file" "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRR}")
220	  z_args+=("--env-file" "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRD}")
221	  z_args+=("--env-file" "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBDC}")
222	  z_args+=("--env-file" "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBJE}")
223	  z_args+=("--env-file" "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN}")
224	  z_args+=("-f" "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE}")
225	
226	  # Include nameplate fragment if it exists
227	  if test -f "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_FRAGMENT}"; then
228	    z_args+=("-f" "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_FRAGMENT}")
229	  fi
230	
231	  z_args+=("-p" "${ZRBOB_PROJECT}")
232	  z_args+=("$@")
233	
234	  buc_log_args "${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} ${z_args[*]}"
235	  "${ZRBOB_RUNTIME}" "${z_args[@]}"
236	}

238	######################################################################
239	# Auto-Summon Helpers
240	
241	# Discriminate kludge hallmarks (local-only, no GAR presence) from conjure /
242	# bind / graft hallmarks. Used by charge preflight to redirect the diagnostic
243	# away from the summon path, which is guaranteed to fail for kludge stamps.
244	zrbob_hallmark_is_kludge() {
245	  case "${1}" in
246	    "${RBGC_HALLMARK_PREFIX_KLUDGE}"*) return 0 ;;
247	    *) return 1 ;;
248	  esac
249	}
250	
251	# Pull all three arks (image, about, vouch) for a hallmark to local runtime.
252	# Used when charge detects a missing local artifact and needs to bootstrap.
253	# Mirrors rbfr_summon's all-three-arks semantics; called inline rather than
254	# as a tabtarget invocation because rbob is the sole caller and avoids the
255	# launcher-dispatch round-trip.
256	zrbob_summon_full_hallmark() {
257	  local z_hallmark="${1:-}"
258	
259	  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "zrbob_summon_full_hallmark: hallmark required"
260	
261	  local z_gar_base="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
262	  local z_image_ref="${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE}:${z_hallmark}"
263	  local z_about_ref="${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_ABOUT}:${z_hallmark}"
264	  local z_vouch_ref="${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH}:${z_hallmark}"
265	
266	  local z_registry_host="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
267	  test -f "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Retriever credential not found: ${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}"
268	
269	  local z_token
270	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to get OAuth token for auto-summon"
271	
272	  buc_step "Auto-summoning hallmark ${z_hallmark} (image + about + vouch)"
273	
274	  rbgo_docker_login "${z_token}" "${z_registry_host}"
275	
276	  docker pull "${z_image_ref}" || buc_die "Failed to pull image ark: ${z_image_ref}"
277	  docker pull "${z_about_ref}" || buc_die "Failed to pull about ark: ${z_about_ref}"
278	  docker pull "${z_vouch_ref}" || buc_die "Failed to pull vouch ark: ${z_vouch_ref}"
279	
280	  buc_info "Auto-summoned: ${z_hallmark}"
281	}
282	
283	# Pull a single image ref to local runtime — used by the image-only auto-summon
284	# branch in rbob_charge as defense-in-depth for partial-state cases (vouch
285	# present but image missing). The full-hallmark helper above covers the common
286	# path where all arks are missing together.
287	# Usage: zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon <vessel> <hallmark> <image_ref>
288	zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon() {
289	  local z_vessel="${1:-}"
290	  local z_hallmark="${2:-}"
291	  local z_image_ref="${3:-}"
292	
293	  test -n "${z_vessel}"       || buc_die "zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon: vessel required"
294	  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon: hallmark required"
295	  test -n "${z_image_ref}"    || buc_die "zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon: image_ref required"
296	
297	  local z_registry_host="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
298	  test -f "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Retriever credential not found: ${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}"
299	
300	  local z_token
301	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to get OAuth token for auto-summon"
302	
303	  # Pull the image
304	  buc_step "Auto-summoning ${z_image_ref}"
305	
306	  rbgo_docker_login "${z_token}" "${z_registry_host}"
307	
308	  docker pull "${z_image_ref}" || buc_die "Failed to pull image: ${z_image_ref}"
309	
310	  buc_info "Auto-summoned: ${z_image_ref}"
311	}

313	######################################################################
314	# Charge Invariant — Subnet-Keyed Reclaim
315	
316	# At most one crucible per nameplate per system. A crucible's subnet derives
317	# from the nameplate (RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP), not from the project prefix, so
318	# two same-nameplate crucibles can never coexist on one daemon. Free the
319	# nameplate's subnet by tearing down whatever network occupies it — regardless
320	# of prefix or how the network was created (compose or manual `docker network
321	# create`). Match on the subnet, a structural fact, not on inferred name
322	# lineage. Our own compose network is excluded; compose-down already handled it.
323	zrbob_reclaim_subnet() {
324	  zrbob_sentinel
325	
326	  local -r z_target_subnet="${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}/${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}"
327	  local -r z_networks_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_reclaim_networks.txt"
328	  local -r z_networks_stderr="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_reclaim_networks_stderr.txt"
329	  local -r z_inspect_prefix="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_reclaim_inspect_"
330	  local -r z_containers_prefix="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_reclaim_containers_"
331	  local -r z_crm_prefix="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_reclaim_crm_"
332	  local -r z_nrm_prefix="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_reclaim_nrm_"
333	
334	  ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} network ls --format '{{.Name}}' \
335	    > "${z_networks_file}" 2>"${z_networks_stderr}" \
336	    || buc_die "Failed to list Docker networks — see ${z_networks_stderr}"
337	
338	  # Load network names (load-then-iterate per BCG; names are captured before any
339	  # reclaim, so removing networks mid-loop cannot disturb the iteration)
340	  local z_names=()
341	  local z_line=""
342	  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
343	    test -n "${z_line}" || continue
344	    z_names+=("${z_line}")
345	  done < "${z_networks_file}"
346	
347	  local z_index=0
348	  local z_name=""
349	  local z_subnet_file=""
350	  local z_subnet_stderr=""
351	  local z_subnet=""
352	  local z_containers_file=""
353	  local z_containers_stderr=""
354	  local z_crm_file=""
355	  local z_nrm_file=""
356	
357	  if (( ${#z_names[@]} )); then
358	    for z_name in "${z_names[@]}"; do
359	      z_subnet_file="${z_inspect_prefix}${z_index}.txt"
360	      z_subnet_stderr="${z_inspect_prefix}${z_index}_stderr.txt"
361	      z_containers_file="${z_containers_prefix}${z_index}.txt"
362	      z_containers_stderr="${z_containers_prefix}${z_index}_stderr.txt"
363	      z_crm_file="${z_crm_prefix}${z_index}.txt"
364	      z_nrm_file="${z_nrm_prefix}${z_index}.txt"
365	      z_index=$((z_index + 1))
366	
367	      # Skip our own compose network — compose down already handled it
368	      test "${z_name}" != "${ZRBOB_NETWORK}" || continue
369	
370	      # A network in the list may vanish or lack IPAM config between ls and
371	      # inspect; tolerate by skipping (stderr preserved for forensics)
372	      ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} network inspect "${z_name}" \
373	        --format '{{range .IPAM.Config}}{{.Subnet}}{{end}}' \
374	        > "${z_subnet_file}" 2>"${z_subnet_stderr}" \
375	        || continue
376	
377	      z_subnet=$(<"${z_subnet_file}")
378	      test "${z_subnet}" = "${z_target_subnet}" || continue
379	
380	      buc_warn "Subnet ${z_target_subnet} occupied by stale network '${z_name}' — reclaiming"
381	
382	      ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} ps -aq --filter "network=${z_name}" \
383	        > "${z_containers_file}" 2>"${z_containers_stderr}" \
384	        || buc_die "Failed to list containers on '${z_name}' — see ${z_containers_stderr}"
385	
386	      local z_containers=()
387	      local z_cline=""
388	      while IFS= read -r z_cline || test -n "${z_cline}"; do
389	        test -n "${z_cline}" || continue
390	        z_containers+=("${z_cline}")
391	      done < "${z_containers_file}"
392	
393	      # Force-remove attached containers first (a mid-run-killed crucible may
394	      # leave them running). Non-fatal: a container may already be exiting; the
395	      # network rm below is the real gate — if removal truly failed there, that
396	      # rm fails and dies.
397	      if (( ${#z_containers[@]} )); then
398	        ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} rm -f "${z_containers[@]}" \
399	          > "${z_crm_file}" 2>&1 \
400	          || buc_warn "Some containers on '${z_name}' resisted removal — see ${z_crm_file}"
401	      fi
402	
403	      ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} network rm "${z_name}" \
404	        > "${z_nrm_file}" 2>&1 \
405	        || buc_die "Failed to reclaim subnet ${z_target_subnet}: could not remove network '${z_name}' — see ${z_nrm_file}"
406	
407	      buc_info "Reclaimed subnet ${z_target_subnet} from '${z_name}'"
408	    done
409	  fi
410	}

412	######################################################################
413	# Public API
414	
415	# Start the crucible (sentry + pentacle + bottle) via compose
416	# Requires: RBOB kindled (which requires RBRN and RBRR)
417	rbob_charge() {
418	  zrbob_sentinel
419	
420	  buc_step "Starting crucible: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
421	
422	  # Gate: nameplate must have no uncommitted changes
423	  if ! git diff --quiet -- "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN}" 2>/dev/null; then
424	    buc_die "Nameplate has uncommitted changes: ${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN} — commit before charging"
425	  fi
426	
427	  # Cross-nameplate validation (silent on success, dies on conflict)
428	  rbrn_preflight
429	
430	  # Preflight: ensure all hallmark arks exist locally. Vouch presence is the
431	  # hallmark-level signal — if vouch is missing, the full hallmark is treated
432	  # as not-yet-summoned and all three arks are pulled together. Image-only
433	  # auto-summon below is defense-in-depth for partial-state cases.
434	  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_SENTRY_VOUCH}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
435	    if zrbob_hallmark_is_kludge "${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"; then
436	      buc_die "Kludge sentry hallmark not built locally: ${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"
437	    fi
438	    buc_warn "Sentry vouch artifact missing locally: ${ZRBOB_SENTRY_VOUCH}"
439	    zrbob_summon_full_hallmark "${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"
440	  fi
441	
442	  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_VOUCH}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
443	    if zrbob_hallmark_is_kludge "${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"; then
444	      buc_die "Kludge bottle hallmark not built locally: ${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"
445	    fi
446	    buc_warn "Bottle vouch artifact missing locally: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_VOUCH}"
447	    zrbob_summon_full_hallmark "${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"
448	  fi
449	
450	  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
451	    if zrbob_hallmark_is_kludge "${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"; then
452	      buc_die "Kludge sentry hallmark not built locally: ${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"
453	    fi
454	    buc_warn "Sentry image not found locally: ${ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE}"
455	    zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon "${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL}" "${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}" "${ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE}"
456	  fi
457	
458	  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
459	    if zrbob_hallmark_is_kludge "${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"; then
460	      buc_die "Kludge bottle hallmark not built locally: ${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"
461	    fi
462	    buc_warn "Bottle image not found locally: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE}"
463	    zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon "${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}" "${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}" "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE}"
464	  fi
465	
466	  # Tear down any prior state (tolerates missing project)
467	  buc_step "Cleaning up any prior state (quenching any preexisting can take a couple of minutes)"
468	  zrbob_compose --profile sessile down --remove-orphans 2>/dev/null || true
469	
470	  # Charge invariant: at most one crucible per nameplate per system. A
471	  # crucible's subnet derives from the nameplate, not the prefix, so freeing
472	  # the nameplate's subnet — tearing down whatever network occupies it,
473	  # regardless of prefix or creation method — is sufficient and subsumes any
474	  # name-lineage collision check.
475	  zrbob_reclaim_subnet
476	
477	  # Start services via compose (--profile sessile includes bottle)
478	  # --wait blocks until all health checks pass (sentry → pentacle → bottle chain)
479	  buc_step "Starting services via compose"
480	  zrbob_compose --profile sessile up -d --wait
481	
482	  buc_step "Crucible started: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
483	
484	  if test "${RBRN_BOTTLE_READINESS_DELAY_SEC}" -gt 0; then
485	    buc_step "Waiting ${RBRN_BOTTLE_READINESS_DELAY_SEC}s for bottle service readiness"
486	    sleep "${RBRN_BOTTLE_READINESS_DELAY_SEC}"
487	  fi
488	
489	  if test -x "${ZRBOB_POST_CHARGE_HOOK}"; then
490	    buc_step "Running post-charge hook: ${ZRBOB_POST_CHARGE_HOOK}"
491	    "${ZRBOB_POST_CHARGE_HOOK}" || buc_die "Post-charge hook failed: ${ZRBOB_POST_CHARGE_HOOK}"
492	  fi
493	}
494	
495	# Check whether the crucible is charged — sentry, pentacle, and bottle must each
496	# be individually `running`. BCG predicate: returns 0 if charged, 1 if not.
497	# Never dies, no output. Compose stderr captured to BURD_TEMP_DIR for operator
498	# inspection on verify-active failures.
499	rbob_charged_predicate() {
500	  zrbob_sentinel
501	
502	  local z_service=""
503	  local z_ids_file=""
504	  local z_stderr_file=""
505	
506	  for z_service in "${RBCC_container_sentry}" "${RBCC_container_pentacle}" "${RBCC_container_bottle}"; do
507	    z_ids_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_charged_${z_service}_ids.txt"
508	    z_stderr_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_charged_${z_service}_stderr.txt"
509	
510	    "${ZRBOB_RUNTIME}" compose -p "${ZRBOB_PROJECT}" ps "${z_service}" --status running -q \

585	# Drive Hallmark — rewrite a single RBRN_*_HALLMARK line in nameplate env
586	# Args: nameplate_file variable_name new_hallmark
587	
588	zrbob_drive_hallmark() {
589	  local -r z_file="$1"
590	  local -r z_var_name="$2"
591	  local -r z_new_value="$3"
592	
593	  test -f "${z_file}" || buc_die "Nameplate file not found: ${z_file}"
594	  test -n "${z_var_name}" || buc_die "Variable name required"
595	  test -n "${z_new_value}" || buc_die "New hallmark value required"
596	
597	  # Load file into array (BCG load-then-iterate)
598	  local z_lines=()
599	  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
600	    z_lines+=("${z_line}")
601	  done < "${z_file}"
602	
603	  # Rewrite with substitution
604	  local -r z_temp="${ZRBOB_DRIVE_PREFIX}${z_var_name}"
605	  : > "${z_temp}" || buc_die "Failed to create temp file: ${z_temp}"
606	
607	  local z_found=0
608	  local z_i="" z_current=""
609	  for z_i in "${!z_lines[@]}"; do
610	    z_current="${z_lines[$z_i]}"
611	    case "${z_current}" in
612	      "${z_var_name}"=*)
613	        z_current="${z_var_name}=${z_new_value}"
614	        z_found=1
615	        ;;
616	    esac
617	    printf '%s\n' "${z_current}" >> "${z_temp}" || buc_die "Failed to write line"
618	  done
619	
620	  test "${z_found}" = "1" || buc_die "Variable ${z_var_name} not found in ${z_file}"
621	
622	  # Atomic replace
623	  mv "${z_temp}" "${z_file}" || buc_die "Failed to replace nameplate file: ${z_file}"
624	
625	  buc_info "Drove ${z_var_name}=${z_new_value} → ${z_file}"
626	}

21	set -euo pipefail

24	test -z "${ZRBRN_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbrn multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"

27	######################################################################
28	# Internal Functions (zrbrn_*)
29	
30	zrbrn_kindle() {
31	  test -z "${ZRBRN_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbrn already kindled"
32	
33	  # No defaults set — buv uses ${!varname:-} for safe indirect expansion under set -u.
34	  # Unset variables are detected distinctly from empty by zbuv_check_capture.
35	
36	  # Enroll all RBRN variables — single source of truth for validation and rendering
37	
38	  buv_regime_enroll RBRN
39	
40	  buv_group_enroll "Core Service Identity"
41	  buv_xname_enroll   RBRN_MONIKER                 2   12  "Unique identifier for Crucible"
42	  buv_string_enroll  RBRN_DESCRIPTION              0  120  "Human-readable description"
43	  buv_enum_enroll    RBRN_RUNTIME                              "Container runtime: docker or podman" \
44	                     docker podman
45	
46	  buv_group_enroll "Container Image Configuration"
47	  buv_fqin_enroll    RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL            1  128  "Vessel identifier for Sentry Image"
48	  buv_fqin_enroll    RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL            1  128  "Vessel identifier for Bottle Image"
49	  buv_fqin_enroll    RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK      1  128  "Hallmark tag for Sentry Image"
50	  buv_fqin_enroll    RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK      1  128  "Hallmark tag for Bottle Image"
51	
52	  buv_group_enroll "Service Readiness"
53	  buv_decimal_enroll RBRN_BOTTLE_READINESS_DELAY_SEC  0  300  "Seconds to wait after compose-up for bottle services to become ready (0 = no wait)"
54	
55	  buv_group_enroll "Entry Service Configuration"
56	  buv_enum_enroll    RBRN_ENTRY_MODE                           "Entry functionality: disabled or enabled" \
57	                     rbnne_disabled rbnne_enabled
58	  buv_gate_enroll    RBRN_ENTRY_MODE  rbnne_enabled
59	  buv_port_enroll    RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION               "External port on Transit Network"
60	  buv_port_enroll    RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE                   "Enclave port between Sentry and Bottle"
61	
62	  buv_group_enroll "Enclave Network Configuration"
63	  buv_ipv4_enroll    RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP                      "Base IPv4 for enclave network"
64	  buv_decimal_enroll RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK          8   30  "Network mask width (8-30)"
65	  buv_ipv4_enroll    RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP                    "IP address for Sentry Container"
66	  buv_ipv4_enroll    RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP                    "IP address for Bottle Container"
67	
68	  buv_group_enroll "Uplink Core"
69	  buv_port_enroll    RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN                      "Minimum port for outbound connections"
70	  buv_enum_enroll    RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE                      "DNS mode: disabled, global, or allowlist" \
71	                     rbnne_disabled rbnne_global rbnne_allowlist
72	  buv_enum_enroll    RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE                   "IP access mode: disabled, global, or allowlist" \
73	                     rbnne_disabled rbnne_global rbnne_allowlist
74	
75	  buv_group_enroll "Uplink DNS Allowlist"
76	  buv_gate_enroll    RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE  rbnne_allowlist
77	  buv_list_domain_enroll RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS           "Allowed DNS domains"
78	
79	  buv_group_enroll "Uplink Access Allowlist"
80	  buv_gate_enroll    RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE  rbnne_allowlist
81	  buv_list_cidr_enroll   RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS             "Allowed CIDR ranges"
82	
83	  # Guard against unexpected RBRN_ variables not in enrollment
84	  buv_scope_sentinel RBRN RBRN_
85	
86	  # Lock all enrolled RBRN_ variables against mutation
87	  buv_lock RBRN
88	
89	  readonly ZRBRN_KINDLED=1
90	}
91	
92	zrbrn_sentinel() {
93	  test "${ZRBRN_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbrn not kindled - call zrbrn_kindle first"
94	}
95	
96	# Enforce all RBRN enrollment validations and custom format checks
97	zrbrn_enforce() {
98	  zrbrn_sentinel
99	
100	  buv_vet RBRN
101	
102	  # Verify IPs fall within declared subnet
103	  zrbrn_ip_in_subnet RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}" "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}" "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}"
104	  zrbrn_ip_in_subnet RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}" "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}" "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}"
105	
106	  # Cross-port check (entry ports must be less than uplink port min)
107	  if test "${RBRN_ENTRY_MODE}" = "rbnne_enabled"; then
108	    test "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}" -lt "${RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN}" || \
109	      buc_die "RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION must be less than RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN"
110	    test "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE}" -lt "${RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN}" || \
111	      buc_die "RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE must be less than RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN"
112	  fi
113	
114	  # Build docker env args array from validated values
115	  # Usage: docker run "${ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV[@]}" ...
116	  buv_docker_env RBRN ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV
117	}
118	
119	######################################################################
120	# Public Functions (rbrn_*)
121	
122	# Source an arbitrary RBRN regime file and run the full kindle->enforce
123	# chain against it, failing on first fault. Test-facing contract surface:
124	# theurge drives synthetic-malformed regime files through this without
125	# reaching module internals. Prerequisite: buv kindled.
126	rbrn_probate() {
127	  local -r z_file="${1:-}"
128	  test -n "${z_file}" || buc_die "rbrn_probate: regime file argument required"
129	  source "${z_file}"  || buc_die "rbrn_probate: cannot source ${z_file}"
130	  zrbrn_kindle
131	  zrbrn_enforce
132	}
133	
134	# List available nameplate monikers as space-separated tokens
135	# Prerequisite: RBCC sourced (needs RBCC_moorings_dir, RBCC_rbrn_file)
136	rbrn_list_capture() {
137	  zrbcc_sentinel
138	
139	  local z_result=""
140	  local z_files=("${RBCC_moorings_dir}/"*"/${RBCC_rbrn_file}")
141	  local z_i=""
142	  for z_i in "${!z_files[@]}"; do
143	    test -f "${z_files[$z_i]}" || continue
144	    local z_dir="${z_files[$z_i]%/*}"
145	    local z_moniker="${z_dir##*/}"
146	    z_result="${z_result}${z_result:+ }${z_moniker}"
147	  done
148	  test -n "${z_result}" || return 1
149	  echo "${z_result}"
150	}
151	
152	######################################################################
153	# Cross-Nameplate Functions
154	#
155	# rbrn_preflight:  Requires RBCC kindled (called from CLI rbrn_audit)
156	
157	# Convert dotted-quad IPv4 to integer for subnet arithmetic
158	zrbrn_ip_to_int() {
159	  local z_a z_b z_c z_d
160	  IFS='.' read -r z_a z_b z_c z_d <<< "$1"
161	  echo $(( (z_a << 24) + (z_b << 16) + (z_c << 8) + z_d ))
162	}
163	
164	# Validate that an IP falls within a subnet (dies if not)
165	# Usage: zrbrn_ip_in_subnet LABEL IP BASE MASK
166	zrbrn_ip_in_subnet() {
167	  local z_label="$1" z_ip="$2" z_base="$3" z_mask="$4"
168	  local z_ip_int=$(zrbrn_ip_to_int "${z_ip}")
169	  local z_base_int=$(zrbrn_ip_to_int "${z_base}")
170	  local z_net_mask=$(( (0xFFFFFFFF << (32 - z_mask)) & 0xFFFFFFFF ))
171	  if [[ $(( z_ip_int & z_net_mask )) -ne $(( z_base_int & z_net_mask )) ]]; then
172	    buc_die "${z_label}=${z_ip} is not within subnet ${z_base}/${z_mask}"
173	  fi
174	}
175	
176	# Cross-nameplate conflict validation (silent on success, dies on conflict)
177	# Checks: port uniqueness, subnet non-overlap, enclave IP uniqueness
178	rbrn_preflight() {
179	  zrbcc_sentinel
180	
181	  # Collect structured data from all nameplates via isolation subshells
182	  local z_nameplate_files=("${RBCC_moorings_dir}/"*"/${RBCC_rbrn_file}")
183	  local z_data_lines=()
184	  local z_nf_i=""
185	  for z_nf_i in "${!z_nameplate_files[@]}"; do
186	    test -f "${z_nameplate_files[$z_nf_i]}" || continue
187	    local z_line
188	    z_line=$(
189	      bash -c '
190	        source "$1" || exit 1
191	        echo "${RBRN_MONIKER}|${RBRN_ENTRY_MODE}|${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION:-0}|${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE:-0}|${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}|${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}|${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}|${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}"
192	      ' _ "${z_nameplate_files[$z_nf_i]}"
193	    ) || buc_die "Preflight isolation failed for: ${z_nameplate_files[$z_nf_i]}"
194	    z_data_lines+=("${z_line}")
195	  done
196	
197	  # Parallel arrays for conflict detection (bash 3.2 compatible)
198	  local z_ws_port_keys=()
199	  local z_ws_port_vals=()
200	  local z_enc_port_keys=()
201	  local z_enc_port_vals=()
202	  local z_ip_keys=()
203	  local z_ip_vals=()
204	  local z_net_starts=()
205	  local z_net_ends=()
206	  local z_net_owners=()
207	
208	  local z_mon=""
209	  local z_entry=""
210	  local z_ws=""
211	  local z_enc=""
212	  local z_base=""
213	  local z_mask=""
214	  local z_sentry=""
215	  local z_bottle=""
216	  for z_nf_i in "${!z_data_lines[@]}"; do
217	    IFS='|' read -r z_mon z_entry z_ws z_enc z_base z_mask z_sentry z_bottle <<< "${z_data_lines[$z_nf_i]}" \
218	      || buc_die "Failed to parse nameplate data line"
219	    test -n "${z_mon}" || continue
220	
221	    # Workstation and enclave port uniqueness (enabled entries only)
222	    if test "${z_entry}" = "rbnne_enabled"; then
223	      local z_i
224	      for z_i in "${!z_ws_port_keys[@]}"; do
225	        if test "${z_ws_port_keys[$z_i]}" = "${z_ws}"; then
226	          buc_die "Port conflict: RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION=${z_ws} claimed by both ${z_ws_port_vals[$z_i]} and ${z_mon}"
227	        fi
228	      done
229	      z_ws_port_keys+=("${z_ws}")
230	      z_ws_port_vals+=("${z_mon}")
231	
232	      # Enclave ports are scoped to their Docker network — key on base_ip:port
233	      local z_enc_key="${z_base}:${z_enc}"
234	      for z_i in "${!z_enc_port_keys[@]}"; do
235	        if test "${z_enc_port_keys[$z_i]}" = "${z_enc_key}"; then
236	          buc_die "Port conflict: RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE=${z_enc} on network ${z_base} claimed by both ${z_enc_port_vals[$z_i]} and ${z_mon}"
237	        fi
238	      done
239	      z_enc_port_keys+=("${z_enc_key}")
240	      z_enc_port_vals+=("${z_mon}")
241	    fi
242	
243	    # Enclave IP uniqueness (all sentry and bottle IPs across nameplates)
244	    local z_j
245	    for z_j in "${!z_ip_keys[@]}"; do
246	      if test "${z_ip_keys[$z_j]}" = "${z_sentry}"; then
247	        buc_die "IP conflict: ${z_sentry} claimed by ${z_mon} (sentry) and ${z_ip_vals[$z_j]}"
248	      fi
249	    done
250	    z_ip_keys+=("${z_sentry}")
251	    z_ip_vals+=("${z_mon}:sentry")
252	
253	    for z_j in "${!z_ip_keys[@]}"; do
254	      if test "${z_ip_keys[$z_j]}" = "${z_bottle}"; then
255	        buc_die "IP conflict: ${z_bottle} claimed by ${z_mon} (bottle) and ${z_ip_vals[$z_j]}"
256	      fi
257	    done
258	    z_ip_keys+=("${z_bottle}")
259	    z_ip_vals+=("${z_mon}:bottle")
260	
261	    # Subnet non-overlap
262	    local z_net_int=$(zrbrn_ip_to_int "${z_base}")
263	    local z_net_mask_bits=$(( (0xFFFFFFFF << (32 - z_mask)) & 0xFFFFFFFF ))
264	    local z_net_addr=$(( z_net_int & z_net_mask_bits ))
265	    local z_net_size=$(( 1 << (32 - z_mask) ))
266	    local z_net_end=$(( z_net_addr + z_net_size - 1 ))
267	
268	    local z_k
269	    for z_k in "${!z_net_starts[@]}"; do
270	      if [[ ${z_net_addr} -le ${z_net_ends[$z_k]} ]] && [[ ${z_net_starts[$z_k]} -le ${z_net_end} ]]; then
271	        buc_die "Subnet overlap: ${z_base}/${z_mask} (${z_mon}) overlaps with network of ${z_net_owners[$z_k]}"
272	      fi
273	    done
274	    z_net_starts+=("${z_net_addr}")
275	    z_net_ends+=("${z_net_end}")
276	    z_net_owners+=("${z_mon}")
277	
278	  done
279	}

19	# Recipe Bottle Regime Vessel - Validator Module
20	
21	set -euo pipefail
22	
23	# Multiple inclusion detection
24	test -z "${ZRBRV_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbrv multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
25	ZRBRV_SOURCED=1
26	
27	######################################################################
28	# Internal Functions (zrbrv_*)
29	
30	zrbrv_kindle() {
31	  test -z "${ZRBRV_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbrv already kindled"
32	
33	  # No defaults set — buv uses ${!varname:-} for safe indirect expansion under set -u.
34	  # Unset variables are detected distinctly from empty by zbuv_check_capture.
35	
36	  # Enroll all RBRV variables — single source of truth for validation and rendering
37	
38	  buv_regime_enroll RBRV
39	
40	  buv_group_enroll "Core Vessel Identity"
41	  buv_xname_enroll  RBRV_SIGIL             1   64  "Unique identifier (must match directory name)"
42	  buv_string_enroll RBRV_DESCRIPTION       0  512  "Human-readable description"
43	  buv_string_enroll RBRV_USER              0   64  "Container runtime user (unset means image default)"
44	  buv_enum_enroll   RBRV_VESSEL_MODE               "Operation mode: bind, conjure, or graft" \
45	                    rbnve_bind rbnve_conjure rbnve_graft
46	
47	  buv_group_enroll "Tool Image Reliquary"
48	  buv_string_enroll RBRV_RELIQUARY             1   14  "Reliquary datestamp (e.g., r260324193326) — identifies tool image set in GAR"
49	
50	  buv_group_enroll "Egress Mode"
51	  buv_enum_enroll   RBRV_EGRESS_MODE                   "Pool routing for primary build operation" \
52	                    rbnve_tether rbnve_airgap
53	
54	  buv_group_enroll "Binding Configuration"
55	  buv_gate_enroll   RBRV_VESSEL_MODE  rbnve_bind
56	  buv_fqin_enroll   RBRV_BIND_IMAGE                1  512  "Source image to copy from registry"
57	  buv_string_enroll RBRV_BIND_OPTIONAL_DOCKERFILE  0  512  "Optional Dockerfile for about recipe.txt"
58	
59	  buv_group_enroll "Conjuring Configuration"
60	  buv_gate_enroll   RBRV_VESSEL_MODE  rbnve_conjure
61	  buv_string_enroll RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE    1  512  "Dockerfile path relative to repo root"
62	  buv_string_enroll RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT    1  512  "Build context relative to repo root"
63	  buv_string_enroll RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS     1  512  "Space-separated target platforms"
64	
65	  buv_group_enroll "Image Group"
66	  buv_gate_enroll   RBRV_VESSEL_MODE  rbnve_conjure
67	  buv_string_enroll RBRV_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN   0  512  "Upstream base image tag slot 1 (e.g., python:3.11-slim)"
68	  buv_string_enroll RBRV_IMAGE_1_ANCHOR   0  512  "GAR-mirrored anchor locator slot 1 (package-path:tag, written by enshrine)"
69	  buv_string_enroll RBRV_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN   0  512  "Upstream base image tag slot 2"
70	  buv_string_enroll RBRV_IMAGE_2_ANCHOR   0  512  "GAR-mirrored anchor locator slot 2 (package-path:tag, written by enshrine)"
71	  buv_string_enroll RBRV_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN   0  512  "Upstream base image tag slot 3"
72	  buv_string_enroll RBRV_IMAGE_3_ANCHOR   0  512  "GAR-mirrored anchor locator slot 3 (package-path:tag, written by enshrine)"
73	
74	  buv_group_enroll "Grafting Configuration"
75	  buv_gate_enroll   RBRV_VESSEL_MODE  rbnve_graft
76	  buv_string_enroll RBRV_GRAFT_IMAGE                1  512  "Local image reference for graft operations"
77	  buv_string_enroll RBRV_GRAFT_OPTIONAL_DOCKERFILE  0  512  "Optional Dockerfile for about recipe.txt"
78	
79	  # Guard against unexpected RBRV_ variables not in enrollment
80	  buv_scope_sentinel RBRV RBRV_
81	
82	  # Lock all enrolled RBRV_ variables against mutation
83	  buv_lock RBRV
84	
85	  readonly ZRBRV_KINDLED=1
86	}
87	
88	zrbrv_sentinel() {
89	  test "${ZRBRV_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbrv not kindled - call zrbrv_kindle first"
90	}
91	
92	# Enforce all RBRV enrollment validations
93	zrbrv_enforce() {
94	  zrbrv_sentinel
95	
96	  buv_vet RBRV
97	}
98	
99	######################################################################
100	# Public Functions (rbrv_*)
101	
102	# Source an arbitrary RBRV regime file and run the full kindle->enforce
103	# chain against it, failing on first fault. Test-facing contract surface:
104	# theurge drives synthetic-malformed regime files through this without
105	# reaching module internals. Prerequisite: buv kindled.
106	rbrv_probate() {
107	  local -r z_file="${1:-}"
108	  test -n "${z_file}" || buc_die "rbrv_probate: regime file argument required"
109	  source "${z_file}"  || buc_die "rbrv_probate: cannot source ${z_file}"
110	  zrbrv_kindle
111	  zrbrv_enforce
112	}
113	
114	# List available vessel sigils as space-separated tokens
115	# Prerequisite: RBRR kindled (needs RBRR_VESSEL_DIR)
116	rbrv_list_capture() {
117	  zrbrr_sentinel
118	
119	  local z_result=""
120	  local z_dirs=("${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}"/*)
121	  local z_i=""
122	  for z_i in "${!z_dirs[@]}"; do
123	    local z_d="${z_dirs[$z_i]}"
124	    test -d "${z_d}" || continue
125	    test -f "${z_d}/rbrv.env" || continue
126	    local z_s="${z_d%/}"
127	    z_result="${z_result}${z_result:+ }${z_s##*/}"
128	  done
129	  test -n "${z_result}" || return 1
130	  echo "${z_result}"
131	}

3	#
4	# Recipe Bottle base compose file — static infrastructure
5	#
6	# Invoked via:
7	#   docker compose --env-file rbmm_moorings/rbrr.env --env-file rbmm_moorings/rbrd.env \
8	#     --env-file rbmm_moorings/{moniker}/rbrn.env \
9	#     -f Tools/rbk/rbob_compose.yml [-f rbmm_moorings/{moniker}/rbnnh_compose.yml] -p {moniker} up -d
10	#
11	# Env-file variables serve dual purpose:
12	#   1. YAML interpolation (${VAR} in this file — image refs, subnet, container names)
13	#   2. Container environment forwarding (bare names in environment: sections)
14	#
15	# Network topology:
16	#   sentry: transit (uplink to internet via bridge) + enclave (down to bottle)
17	#     Interface naming and default-route assignment are NOT controlled by compose:
18	#       - eth0/eth1 ordering is Docker-engine-defined (sentry IP-discovers via RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP)
19	#       - default route is assigned to the alphabetically-first attached network's gateway
20	#         (compose 'priority:' keys empirically do NOT influence this on linux Docker
21	#         engine 28.x or mac Docker Desktop 28.x — sentry replaces the default route
22	#         with one via uplink at RBJp1, see rbjs_sentry.sh)
23	#   pentacle: enclave only (static IP, routes through sentry)
24	#   bottle: shares pentacle network namespace (network_mode: service:pentacle)
25	#
26	# Health chain: sentry healthy → pentacle starts → pentacle healthy → bottle starts (sessile profile)
27	
28	services:
29	  sentry:
30	    image: ${RBRD_GCP_REGION}-docker.pkg.dev/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}/rbi_hm/${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}/image:${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}
31	    container_name: ${RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX}${RBRN_MONIKER}-sentry
32	    privileged: true
33	    networks:
34	      transit: {}
35	      enclave:
36	        ipv4_address: ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}
37	    ports:
38	      - "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}:${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}"
39	    environment:
40	      - RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP
41	      - RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK
42	      - RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP
43	      - RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP
44	      - RBRR_DNS_SERVER
45	      - RBRN_ENTRY_MODE
46	      - RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION
47	      - RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE
48	      - RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE
49	      - RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN
50	      - RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE
51	      - RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS
52	      - RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS
53	      - RBJE_PROBE
54	    healthcheck:
55	      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "test -f /tmp/rbjh_healthy"]
56	      interval: 2s
57	      timeout: 5s
58	      retries: 15
59	      start_period: 5s
60	
61	  pentacle:
62	    image: ${RBRD_GCP_REGION}-docker.pkg.dev/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}/rbi_hm/${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}/image:${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}
63	    container_name: ${RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX}${RBRN_MONIKER}-pentacle
64	    privileged: true
65	    networks:
66	      enclave:
67	        ipv4_address: ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}
68	    environment:
69	      - RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP
70	    command: ["/opt/rbk/rbjp_pentacle.sh"]
71	    depends_on:
72	      sentry:
73	        condition: service_healthy
74	    healthcheck:
75	      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "test -f /tmp/rbjph_healthy"]
76	      interval: 2s
77	      timeout: 5s
78	      retries: 15
79	      start_period: 5s
80	
81	  bottle:
82	    image: ${RBRD_GCP_REGION}-docker.pkg.dev/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}/rbi_hm/${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}/image:${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}
83	    container_name: ${RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX}${RBRN_MONIKER}-bottle
84	    network_mode: "service:pentacle"
85	    security_opt:
86	      - label:disable
87	    cap_drop:
88	      - NET_RAW
89	      - NET_ADMIN
90	    environment:
91	      - RBOB_HOST_UID
92	      - RBOB_HOST_GID
93	      - RBRV_USER
94	    depends_on:
95	      pentacle:
96	        condition: service_healthy
97	    profiles:
98	      - sessile
99	
100	networks:
101	  transit:
102	    driver: bridge
103	  enclave:
104	    driver: bridge
105	    ipam:
106	      config:
107	        - subnet: ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}/${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}
